essence: Moom has become buggy for positioning Ecamm Live windows across David's 30"/49" monitors, so he asked for research on Moom's company/pricing ('Moom Classic') while leaning toward replacing it with a code-integrated window manager like Hammerspoon.

actions.text: Research Moom company, pricing, and Moom Classic status vs Hammerspoon alternative

essence: David specified a new Gather-skill command that concatenates a folder of JSON into a transient, auto-refreshable markdown doc (with diagram-intent and style-prompt header) for NotebookLM visualization, debriefed Jan's successful Byron branding session (one-liner-the-day-before meeting protocol agreed), and asked for Rectangle/Moom settings extraction across the old M4 Pro and current M4 Mini.

essence: David scoped phase two of the Moments That Matter demo app: recipe-based CRUD over per-record JSON files in a root-level data folder for companies/sites/users/participants, a top-right focus-employee selector that implies the focus company, and moments-that-matter entry per selected employee — built as one background task after a git commit.

essence: David decided indie-dev-dan is reference data, not a standalone brain: transcripts move to the upstream/tubescripts git area (generalizable to other transcript groupings), the '-review' suffix gets dropped, tools get a small markdown doc as future skill candidates, and example/recipe data quality gets verified — plus he iterated on a sub-50-word hand-drawn infographic prompt for Nano Banana.

actions.text: Migrate indie-dev-dan transcripts to upstream/tubescripts, drop the 'review' name, and verify system health after

essence: David wants the pattern-visualization app's setup collapsed to a single install command asking only port and app name, output written to a temp folder auto-opened in Finder, clarity on which NPM publishing steps can be automated versus need him in the loop, plus extraction of the GAVR/Gather JSON structures powering the visualizations and a handover doc for the next session.

actions.text: Design one-command install flow (port + app name) writing to a temp folder opened in Finder
Determine which NPM publish steps automate fully and surface the human-in-the-loop points

essence: Quick check on the EpiStack (AppyStack) plugin: whether recipes use absolute paths, what the plugin is designed to do and where it writes data, and whether its JSON is generated by the plugin itself or by a Gather command.

Sunday Zoom (David/Jan/Voss) tightened Byron the branding agent's lane — test GSB studio-view and TV project-view commands, keep him off storytelling and instead capture a future storyteller agent (concept/role/names) in a suggestive Next Agent file — with David praising Jan's reflection as teaching-grade, coaching Voss on editor save/undo, locking the recurring Sunday meeting, and noting two Looms posted to the AI-TLDR Skool group on the agentic OS and personal agent stack.

Test Byron GSB (studio view) and TV (project view) commands and fix the misspelled TV markdown file

essence: David refined AppyStack recipe doctrine: don't over-fit patterns to AppyStack (others will reuse them), the random alphanumeric slug is the primary key making name-based filenames safely renameable, the 'prepared' pattern generalizes to cross-system knowledge transfer, local-app-then-publish-to-central-DB suits enterprise screen prototyping, and the UI-shell + file-CRUD recipes must land in AppyStack docs as first-install-discoverable recipes with a child folder of sample-data scenarios (SupportSignal, YouTube launch optimizer).

actions.text: Write discoverable UI-shell and file-CRUD recipe docs with sample-data scenario child folder

essence: David designed two foundational AppyStack recipes — a domain-agnostic visual shell (collapsible left menu, header, top-right cog, dynamic context submenus) and JSON file-based persistence using per-entity files (lowercased dash-notation names) so concurrent git edits avoid merge conflicts — distinguishing system vs domain entities via a SupportSignal-style example, plus composite recipes and a new 'prepare' pattern for priming one system with another's knowledge.

actions.text: Deep-research AppyStack architecture and draft visual-shell + file-persistence recipes plus at least two more common UI patterns

essence: Reviewing prompt-pattern work on recipes (prompts that let people adapt an existing system without knowing how to phrase requests), David identified 'discovery prompts' — prompts that let an agent, not a human, deeply learn another system's semantics, workflows, and rationale — as a missing pattern needing research alongside recipes.

actions.text: Research discovery-prompt and recipe prompt patterns and expand the framework if warranted

essence: David and Joy discussed a slow but capable shop employee who lacks confidence, agreeing Joy should tell her she's trusted and appreciated while encouraging her to get faster.

At Joy's juice shop David confirmed coconut drink prices in baht and probed fruit-freezing quality practices — whether passion fruit freezes whole or pulped first — noting each fruit needs its own technique.
